2020-01-25.title: 75 Years After Auschwitz Liberation, Worry That ‘Never Again’ Is Not Assured
2020-01-25.abstract: Amid a surge of anti-Semitism and a rise in dehumanizing political rhetoric, there is fear that the horrific lessons of the death camp are being lost.

2020-01-26.title: North Korean Leader’s Aunt Re-emerges After Husband’s Execution
2020-01-26.abstract: Kim Kyong-hui resurfaces as Kim Jong-un emphasizes his bloodline to consolidate his people’s loyalty.

2020-01-26.title: More U.S. Coronavirus Cases Emerge, as China’s Death Toll Rises
2020-01-26.abstract: The number of dead climbed to at least 80, while in the United States, five cases were confirmed.

2020-01-26.title: France Judges Dead Jihadists but Refuses to Repatriate the Living
2020-01-26.abstract: With public opinion firmly against allowing the return of those who left to fight in Syria and Iraq, the French justice system finds itself facing the strange concept of the “ghost trial.”

2020-01-26.title: Anatomy of a Lie: How Iran Covered Up the Downing of an Airliner
2020-01-26.abstract: For three days, Iranian military officials knew they had shot down a Ukrainian jetliner while the government issued false statements, denying any responsibility.

2020-01-26.title: California Confirms Coronavirus Patient, Marking Third U.S. Case
2020-01-26.abstract: The other two cases were in Washington State and Chicago.

2020-01-27.title: Italy Election Deals Blow to Nationalist Leader Salvini
2020-01-27.abstract: Regional voting thwarted an effort by Matteo Salvini to set off the government’s collapse and return to power as head of the anti-migrant League party.

2020-01-27.title: Alarm Grows as Markets Tumble and Death Toll Rises
2020-01-27.abstract: Health officials in the United States said more than 100 people were being evaluated for possible infection. Of the nearly 3,000 people who have contracted the virus, the vast majority live in China.

2020-01-27.title: Wary Residents Return to Homes Near Volcano in Philippines
2020-01-27.abstract: Hundreds of thousands of people were displaced by the threat of huge eruptions from the Taal Volcano, but officials say the danger has subsided.

2020-01-27.title: Before the Liberation of Auschwitz, a March of Misery
2020-01-27.abstract: On the roads where thousands of prisoners from Auschwitz were forced to march before the camp was liberated, there are few memorials. Mostly graves.

2020-01-27.title: A Knight in Gucci Armor Helps Charge a Geothermal Dragon
2020-01-27.abstract: A company wants to build a geothermal plant in Umbria. Locals — and celebrities who live there — don’t want it.

2020-01-28.title: Coronavirus: Death Toll Climbs, and So Does the Number of Infections
2020-01-28.abstract: The number of known cases of the new virus rose by nearly a third overnight. A shortage of test kits has led experts to warn that the real number may be higher.

2020-01-28.title: Asylum Seekers Find a New Route to Europe, Flowing Into a Divided Cyprus
2020-01-28.abstract: The tiny island now hosts the most refugees per capita in the European Union.

2020-01-28.title: A Divided Hong Kong Confronts the Arrival of the Coronavirus
2020-01-28.abstract: Hong Kong announced the shutdown of major rail passenger links and limits on flights coming from the mainland. China will stop issuing visas for individual travelers to the territory.

2020-01-28.title: Netanyahu Withdraws Immunity Bid, Shifting Election Battleground
2020-01-28.abstract: The Israeli prime minister will face voters on March 2 as a defendant in a criminal case, but without weeks of potentially humiliating parliamentary discussion on the subject.

2020-01-28.title: Russia Exerts Growing Influence in Africa, Worrying Many in the West
2020-01-28.abstract: The Kremlin is increasing arms sales, security pacts and training programs as the American defense secretary weighs withdrawing troops from the continent.

2020-01-29.title: A Deal That Has Two Elections, Rather Than Mideast Peace, as Its Focus
2020-01-29.abstract: The Israeli-Palestinian peace plan unveiled by President Trump and Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu sounds more like a road map for their own futures than for the Middle East.

2020-01-29.title: A Muted Arab Response to Trump’s Mideast Peace Plan
2020-01-29.abstract: The U.S. is banking on Arab leaders to help make its plan for ending the Israeli-Palestinian conflict work. It is not clear how realistic that is.

2020-01-29.title: More Than 7,700 Cases of Coronavirus Recorded Worldwide
2020-01-29.abstract: Chinese officials have confirmed over 7,700 cases of the mysterious illness as foreign governments airlifted their citizens out of Wuhan, the outbreak’s epicenter.

2020-01-29.title: E.U. Recommends Limiting, but Not Banning, Huawei in 5G Rollout
2020-01-29.abstract: The bloc’s experts suggested members limit and monitor the involvement of “high-risk” vendors as they invest in next-generation mobile communications infrastructure.

2020-01-29.abstract: The loss of such a rich, sizable, powerful member state will weaken the European Union’s momentum and its diplomatic weight.

2020-01-30.title: Mexican Butterfly Conservationist Is Found Dead, Two Weeks After Vanishing
2020-01-30.abstract: The cause of death has not been determined, but criminal groups have often attacked environmentalists whose work impedes their interests.

2020-01-30.title: In India, Protesters and Modi Tussle Over Who Can Claim Gandhi
2020-01-30.abstract: Prime Minister Narendra Modi says the national icon would have supported his contentious citizenship law. Demonstrators say it goes against everything Gandhi stood for.

2020-01-30.title: Deaths Surpass 200, and State Department Urges Against Travel to China
2020-01-30.abstract: The United States set its advisory at Level 4, which represents the highest safety risk. The World Health Organization said the virus represents a risk outside of China.

2020-01-30.title: Duterte Orders Cabinet Not to Visit U.S. After Official’s Visa Is Denied
2020-01-30.abstract: The Philippine president issued the order after one of the architects of his bloody drug war was denied a visa for the United States.

2020-01-30.title: Longest Smuggling Tunnel Is Found at U.S.-Mexico Border
2020-01-30.abstract: The shaft ran for four-fifths of a mile between Tijuana and San Diego and was equipped with rails, ventilation, a drainage system and even an elevator, officials said.

2020-01-31.title: More American Troops Sustain Brain Injuries From Iran Missile Strike in Iraq
2020-01-31.abstract: The number, which has steadily increased since the Jan. 8 attack, is now at 64.

2020-01-31.title: State Department Warns Against Traveling to China Amid Coronavirus Outbreak
2020-01-31.abstract: Americans who are currently in China should consider leaving, United States officials said.

2020-01-31.title: Britain’s Brexit Shrug: Let’s Just Get On With It
2020-01-31.abstract: For the last three years, Britons have debated Brexit. Now that it is here, there is a sense of relief even though the country still must decide what sort of Brexit it wants.

2020-01-31.title: What’s Next for Britons Who Lost the Brexit Battle
2020-01-31.abstract: Having bet it all on stopping Brexit and lost, Remainers now look to keeping Britain as close to the European Union as possible.

2020-01-31.title: As Virus Spreads, U.S. Temporarily Bars Foreigners Who’ve Visited China
2020-01-31.abstract: Most foreign nationals will not be allowed to enter the United States if they’ve been to China in the preceding two weeks.
